chunking: atomic (one chunk per fact/FAQ, optimized for dense retrieval)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The official institutional designation is Vishwashanti Gurukul World School.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The 'World School' suffix is a controlled branding measure to distinguish the institution from other MAEER-managed entities and from schools operating under CBSE or other curricula.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The 'World School' designation is a primary indicator of the school's status as a full IB Continuum institution.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The school is owned and governed by the Maharashtra Academy of Engineering and Educational Research (MAEER).

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The MAEER Trust registered office is Survey No. 124, Kothrud Paud Road, Ex-Servicemen Colony, Pune-411 038.

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The institutional physical address is At. Rajbaug, Loni Kalbhor, District Pune.

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The school is located on a 125-acre green campus.

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The campus infrastructure supports comprehensive day and residential educational programs.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The school is an International Day and Residential School.

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The current Head of School is Dr. Vidhukesh Vimal.

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The primary pedagogical framework is the International Baccalaureate (IB).

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The Sanskrit motto on the official logo is: tejasvinavadhitamastu (Devanagari script on logo).

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The campus infrastructure supports 20+ sports disciplines.

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): Vishwashanti Gurukul World School is a formally authorized IB Continuum School.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): IB Continuum status confirms authority to deliver the full suite of IB programmes across all age groups.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The Primary Years Programme (PYP) was originally authorized in April 2009; the certificate was reissued in November 2025.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The Middle Years Programme (MYP) was originally authorized in April 2018; the certificate was reissued in November 2025; it is authorized for Years 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The Diploma Programme (DP) was originally authorized in May 2006; the certificate was reissued in November 2025.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The Career-related Programme (CP) was authorized in October 2025.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): All reissued IB certificates are signed by Olli-Pekka Heinonen, Director General (Geneva).

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The Diploma Programme (May 2006) is the earliest IB programme authorized at the school.

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The school offers four distinct IB programmes: PYP, MYP, DP, and CP.

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The school is authorized for IB for Standard I through Standard XII.

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The foundational benefits of the IB Continuum are a visionary approach to education and holistic student development.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The institution operates under a No Objection Certificate (NOC) for the IB curriculum issued by the School Education and Sports Department, Government of Maharashtra.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The NOC reference number is NOC-4023/C.R.No.263/SM-3.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): The NOC was issued on January 12, 2024 and expires on January 11, 2027 (valid for three years).

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 1: Adherence to Sections 18 and 19 of the Right of Children to Free and Compulsory Education Act, 2009.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 2: Strict adherence to Section 12(1)(C) of the RTE Act, 2009 and Maharashtra RTE Rules 2011 (reserved seats).

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 3: Mandatory integration of 'Divyang' (Special Needs) students in every class, per Resolution No. Mise 2015/(119-A)/SD-6 dated 8th January 2016.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 4: Compliance with all state Educational Acts, Rules, and Policies issued periodically.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 5: Mandatory use of State Board or respective Board curriculum for Standard I to VIII.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 6: Compulsory instruction of the Marathi language as a subject from Standard I to X.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 7: Staff salaries must meet or exceed Schedule 'C' of the Maharashtra Employees of Private Schools (Conditions of Service) Regulation Act, 1981.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 8: Compliance with traffic/safety guidelines and fire protection codes per the National Building Code of India, Part-IV Fire 1997.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 9: Mandatory conduct of 3 tests annually for students up to Standard VIII, with achievement data recorded in the SARAL database.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 10: Regular child-wise data entry into SARAL and U-DISE as demanded by State Authorities.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 11: Compliance with the Maharashtra Educational Institutions (Regulation of Fees) Act, 2011.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 12: Compulsory teaching of Indian History and specifically History related to Maharashtra.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 13: Formation of a Complaint Redressal Committee per the Woman and Child Department government resolution dated 19.6.2014.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 14: The fee structure must be published on the official Government RTE portal.

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School (VGWS): NOC Condition 15: Compulsory entry of student Aadhar Numbers into the SARAL portal.

chunks.text: Q: What is the full official nomenclature of the institution? A: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School. (Vishwashanti Gurukul World School FAQ)

chunks.text: Q: Why was the 'World School' suffix added to the branding? A: To clarify the school's status as an IB Continuum institution and prevent confusion with MAEER's CBSE or other MIT-branded entities. (Vishwashanti Gurukul World School FAQ)

chunks.text: Q: What is the significance of the 'Dynamic Schools 2024' award? A: It recognizes exemplary contribution to education in Maharashtra. (Vishwashanti Gurukul World School FAQ)

chunks.text: Q: How many sports disciplines are supported by the campus infrastructure? A: 20+ sports. (Vishwashanti Gurukul World School FAQ)

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Q: How does the school ensure safety compliance? A: Following guidelines from Police, Fire Services, and the Transport Department. (Vishwashanti Gurukul World School FAQ)

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Q: Does the school have a sexual harassment policy? A: Yes, a Complaint Redressal Committee is formed as per state mandates. (Vishwashanti Gurukul World School FAQ)

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Q: Is student Aadhar registration required? A: Yes, registration on the SARAL portal is mandatory. (Vishwashanti Gurukul World School FAQ)

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Q: What is the Sanskrit motto on the official logo? A: tejasvinavadhitamastu (rendered in Devanagari on the official logo). (Vishwashanti Gurukul World School FAQ)

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)

chunks.text: Q: Who presented the 2022 Hyderabad award? A: Mr. Augus P Saptoo and Shri Navin Mittal. (Vishwashanti Gurukul World School FAQ)

metadata.source: Vishwashanti Gurukul World School: Comprehensive Master Source of Truth (2022-2026)
